free_text: Charged-Particle-Induced Fission: A Mass
Spectrometric Yield Study
Decay data for all
nuclides except 155Eu that was measured in the present
work.
Neutron data.
60- and 184-inch cyclotrons.
A single-directional focusing mass
spectrometer with a 60-degree sector and 12-inch
radius of curvature.
A 235U target was irradiated in the
thermal column of the LPTR at Livermore Radiation
Laboratory. The irradiation time was 3 days with a
neutron flux of 6*10**11 neutrons/cm2/sec. The
purification procedure was as follows: Chemically
pure uranium foil was dissolved in concentrated HCl
and a few drops of concentrated HNO3. After the
target was dissolved, the solution was allowed to
cool and divided into two equal samples. To one
sample was added an accurate amount of certain
separated stable isotopes of elements to be measured.
The solution were adjusted and then passed through
two separate anion-exchange columns. The elutants
were then evaporated almost to dryness. Finally, the
individual rare earths were separated and measured
using the mass spectrometer.
The corrections are both for decay and for neutron
capture. Since the neutron flux used was rather low,
and the irradiation time was rather short, no capture
correction exceeded 1%.
BP
Purified U3O8 of natural uranium. The target was made
by pressing the U3O8 powder into a trough on a copper
piece which was made to fit the accelerator target
holder.
The limits of error are purely statistical;
they are all standard deviations of the measurements.
